meta.name: Vietnam
meta.title: Vietnam Tours & Holiday Packages from Malaysia | Dhesu Travel
meta.description: Discover Vietnam's best kept secrets with expert-guided tours. From Hanoi's bustling markets to Halong Bay's limestone karsts, explore North, Central, South Vietnam, Phu Quoc, and Dalat. Trusted since 1988.

hero.headline: Vietnam Unveiled:
hero.headlineAccent: From Ancient Traditions to Modern Wonders.
hero.body: Halong Bay's limestone karsts rise from emerald water at sunrise. Hoi An's lanterns glow gold over the ancient river. Hanoi's Old Quarter hums through centuries of Buddhist temples and French colonial streets. Vietnam is one country, a dozen worlds — and every one of them is within reach of Malaysia.

why.heading: Five Destinations. One Country That Never Runs Out of New Things.
why.subheading: North, Central, and South Vietnam each feel like a different country. Phu Quoc is the island detour everyone loves. Nha Trang is the beach city on every return trip.

highlights.title: North Vietnam: Halong Bay, Hanoi & Sapa
highlights.body: 7 packages from RM688. Halong Bay's 2,000 limestone karsts rise from UNESCO-listed emerald water — cruise at sunrise, kayak through hidden lagoons. Hanoi's Old Quarter and water puppet theatre. Sapa's terraced rice fields and the Fansipan cable car to Vietnam's highest summit at 3,147 metres.

highlights.title: Central Vietnam: Danang, Hue & Hoi An
highlights.body: 4 packages from RM688. Hoi An Ancient Town — UNESCO-listed merchant port with hundreds of coloured lanterns over the Thu Bon River. Hue's Imperial Citadel and Royal Tombs. Ba Na Hills Golden Bridge suspended in the clouds above Danang. The scenic coastal train between Danang and Hue.

highlights.title: South Vietnam: Ho Chi Minh, Dalat & Mui Ne
highlights.body: 5 packages from RM1,388. Ho Chi Minh City's War Remnants Museum, Cu Chi Tunnels, and best street food in Southeast Asia. Mekong Delta sampan boats and floating markets. Dalat — the City of Eternal Spring — with Asia's longest alpine coaster and flower gardens. Mui Ne's towering sand dunes and beachfront resorts.

highlights.title: Phu Quoc & Nha Trang: Island & Beach
highlights.body: Phu Quoc: 3 packages from RM688 — the world's longest sea cable car, snorkelling on pristine reefs, island hopping, and Duong Dong Night Market seafood. Nha Trang: Vietnam's premier beach city with cobalt-blue water, mud baths at Thap Ba, and some of the best diving in Southeast Asia. Custom packages available.

zones.heading: Five Completely Different Vietnams. One Country.
zones.subheading: Vietnam stretches 1,650 kilometres from Hanoi's ancient lakes to Phu Quoc's tropical waters. 7 packages in the North, 5 in the South, 4 through Central Vietnam, 3 on Phu Quoc — and Nha Trang on request.

areas.name: North Vietnam
areas.description: 7 packages from RM688. Hanoi's Old Quarter temples, water puppetry and French colonial streets. UNESCO Halong Bay limestone karsts by overnight cruise. Sapa's terraced rice fields, hill tribe villages, and Fansipan cable car — Vietnam's highest peak.

areas.name: Central Vietnam
areas.description: 4 packages from RM688. Hoi An Ancient Town's lantern-lit streets and Japanese Covered Bridge. Hue's Imperial Citadel and Royal Tombs UNESCO site. Ba Na Hills Golden Bridge. Danang's Marble Mountains and pristine My Khe Beach.

areas.name: South Vietnam
areas.tag: Ho Chi Minh · Dalat · Mui Ne
areas.description: 5 packages from RM1,388. Ho Chi Minh City's War Remnants Museum and Cu Chi Tunnels. Mekong Delta sampan boats and floating markets. Dalat's City of Eternal Spring with Asia's longest alpine coaster. Mui Ne's sand dunes and beachfront resorts.

areas.name: Phu Quoc
areas.description: 3 packages from RM688. Asia's most talked-about tropical island. World's longest sea cable car (3.3 km). Pristine beaches for snorkelling and water sports. Island hopping to smaller atolls. Duong Dong Night Market for fresh seafood at sunset.

areas.name: Nha Trang
areas.description: Vietnam's premier beach city on the South China Sea. Cobalt-blue water, white sand, and some of the best diving and snorkelling in Southeast Asia. Mud baths at Thap Ba, vinpearl cable car, and a chain of offshore islands. Custom packages on request.

bestTime.heading: Best Time to Visit Vietnam from Malaysia
bestTime.body: Vietnam has two separate monsoon systems affecting north and south differently, so the best time depends on which region you visit. Generally, autumn and spring are ideal for most destinations.

seasons.name: Autumn Season
seasons.description: Ideal season for all Vietnam regions. Cool temperatures 20-30°C, minimal rain, and clear skies perfect for Halong Bay cruises and Sapa hiking. The most popular travel window from Malaysia.

seasons.name: Spring Season
seasons.description: Dalat's flower gardens are at their peak. Mild temperatures ideal for exploration. Northern regions accessible as winter ends. Perfect for trekking and outdoor activities across all regions.

seasons.name: Winter
seasons.description: Cooler temperatures in the north (10-20°C), dry season in the south. Peak season for Ho Chi Minh City and Phu Quoc. Sapa may see occasional snow. Ideal for southern Vietnam beach trips.

seasons.name: Summer Monsoon
seasons.description: Lush monsoon scenery with dramatic green mountains and rice fields. Higher temperatures and humidity, occasional rain in the north. Best rates for budget travellers. Ideal for Phu Quoc island hopping.

tripLength.heading: How Many Days Do You Actually Need?

options.label: Quick Escape
options.description: Enough for a single region — Halong Bay from Hanoi, or a Hoi An-Danang coastal break, or Phu Quoc island. Perfect for a long weekend from Malaysia.

options.label: Sweet Spot
options.description: The recommended duration for North or Central Vietnam. Hanoi plus Halong Bay overnight cruise, or Danang with Hue and Hoi An. Enough to see the highlights without rushing.

options.label: Best of Vietnam
options.description: Combine North and South Vietnam, or add Sapa highlands to the Halong Bay circuit. The longer you have, the more regional variety you can experience.

packages.heading: Vietnam Packages for Every Traveller
packages.subheading: From Halong Bay luxury cruises and Hoi An cultural escapes to Phu Quoc beach breaks and Sapa mountain treks. Every package includes airport transfers and Dhesu's trusted on-ground team.
packages.ctaNote: Custom and group packages available on request.

whyBook.heading: Why Book Your Vietnam Journey Through Dhesu
whyBook.body: Southeast Asia travel rewards those who know the difference between a good Halong Bay cruise operator and a mediocre one, which Hoi An cooking class is authentic and which is a tourist trap, and exactly which seat on the train from Hanoi to Sapa has the mountain view. We have been sending Malaysian travellers to Vietnam since 1988 — that experience shows in every itinerary.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.description: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We assist with Vietnam e-Visa documentation guidance for all packages.

points.title: 35+ Years of Southeast Asia Expertise
points.description: Since 1988, we have been arranging Vietnam journeys for Malaysian travellers. We know which cruise lines deliver, which guides speak real English, and which routes work best in each season.

points.title: Expert Local Guides
points.description: English-speaking local guides who understand Vietnamese history, culture, and the on-the-ground logistics that make the difference between a good trip and a great one.

points.title: Group, Custom & Family Packages
points.description: All packages can be arranged for groups, families, and custom itineraries. Airport transfers, guides, and hotels are included in every package.

cta.heading: Plan Your Vietnam Journey
cta.subheading: Tell us which Vietnam calls to you. We have been arranging the details since 1988.
